tool_design.role_in_intervention: Tracks whether every bought-in product has current allergen evidence and shows which supplier records need chasing before safe sale.
tool_design.usage_moment: Supplier onboarding, product substitution, weekly evidence review, and quarterly audit prep
tool_design.expected_output: Current supplier evidence tracker showing verified, pending, overdue, and blocked products

failure_warnings.warning: The register becomes a filing list instead of an active chase-and-status tracker.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Missing evidence stays hidden until a complaint, audit, or product change exposes it.
early_signals: Products are listed with blank review dates
Managers cannot tell which items are pending versus verified
prevention: Use explicit status values: Verified, Pending, Overdue, Blocked
Review the register weekly against current stock
mitigation: Prioritise high-risk or top-selling bought-in items first
Block sale to allergy-sensitive customers until evidence is current

failure_warnings.warning: Teams assume a new delivery matches the old declaration without verifying product code or revision.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: A supplier reformulation enters service under the old allergen call.
early_signals: Packaging differs from prior deliveries but the register is unchanged
Alternative product codes appear on invoices with no review record
prevention: Match supplier evidence to exact product code and pack size where possible
Log every substitute or reformulation through the change gate
mitigation: Quarantine the product until evidence is checked
Update linked recipe sheets and the matrix from the new declaration

content:
# Supplier Allergen Evidence Register

**Tool ID:** T005-03  
**Block:** B005 (Allergen Identification & Labelling)  
**Format:** Tracker  
**Primary Role:** Manager  
**Use Moment:** Supplier onboarding, weekly evidence review, and change control  
**Output:** Live tracker of bought-in product allergen evidence status

---

## What This Tool Does

This register tells you which bought-in products are backed by current allergen evidence and which ones are not. It should make weak spots visible before they reach customers.

Status key:
- `Verified` = current declaration held and checked
- `Pending` = requested, awaiting supplier response
- `Overdue` = request not resolved by review date
- `Blocked` = do not rely on this product for allergen-safe sale until resolved

---

## Supplier Summary

| Supplier | Contact | Product Types Supplied | Last Evidence Review | Overall Status | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | |
| | | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | |
| | | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | |
| | | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | |

---

## Product Evidence Register

| Product | Supplier | Product Code / Pack | Current Spec Held? | Evidence Date | Next Review | Status | Linked Menu Items | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | Yes / No | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | | |
| | | | Yes / No | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | | |
| | | | Yes / No | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | | |
| | | | Yes / No | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | | |
| | | | Yes / No | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | | |
| | | | Yes / No | | | Verified / Pending / Overdue / Blocked | | |

---

## Evidence Chase Log

| Date | Supplier / Product | Missing or Unclear Information | Action Taken | Next Follow-Up | Escalation Needed? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | | Yes / No |
| | | | | | Yes / No |
| | | | | | Yes / No |
| | | | | | Yes / No |

---

## Weekly Review Checks

<input type="checkbox" disabled /> Every bought-in product currently in use appears in the register  
<input type="checkbox" disabled /> Any substitute or emergency purchase is logged  
<input type="checkbox" disabled /> Overdue evidence has an owner and chase date  
<input type="checkbox" disabled /> Blocked items are not presented as verified in the matrix  
<input type="checkbox" disabled /> Linked menu items are updated if a declaration changes

---

## Common Failure Patterns

### 1. Filing the PDF but never updating the register
The file is not enough. The register is what makes the gap visible during review.

### 2. Treating similar products as identical
Two chocolate syrups from different brands can have different allergen declarations. Track the exact product.

### 3. Letting an overdue item stay in service without any flag
If evidence is overdue, the matrix and customer-facing pack must reflect that uncertainty or the product must be blocked.
